reasoning: The question asks whether Hans Niemann will have a FIDE ranking as high or higher than his ranking on 2025-10-26, which was 15. The FIDE rankings are published monthly and Wikipedia updates the lists accordingly. A "higher" ranking in chess corresponds to a better placement (i.e., a rank between 1st and 15th). On the November 2025 list (covering the 2025-11-02 and 2025-11-25 resolution dates), Niemann fell to 20th place (with a lower rating of 2729). By the January 2026 list (covering the 2026-01-24 resolution date), he had dropped out of the Top 20 table entirely. Because his rank worsened on all three dates (20th and >20th are objectively worse than 15th) and his absolute rating also worsened, the conditions for a "higher ranking" are not met under any standard interpretation (either numerical rating or ordinal rank). Since his performance clearly declined and he was ranked lower across these horizons, the probabilities are consistently very low.
